utility_evaluation.REASONING:
The required format has 6 items: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. The final solution includes all 6 items. Now check if each is fully specified with all details: 
- property: includes selected property with details and price. It has a name, some details, and a price. Although it has a placeholder note, it still provides details and price. Considered fully specified.
- financing: requires financing solution with terms. It lists a solution (Traditional Mortgage 80% LTV) and terms (CAD $1200 arrangement fee). Lacks interest rate, amortization, down payment breakdown, but the required format only asks for solution with terms, so this is acceptable.
- legal_services: requires legal services with costs. Provided: package name and costs. Fully specified per format.
- moving_services: requires moving arrangements with costs. Provided: package and costs. Fully specified per format.
- setup_services: requires utility and setup services. Provided: package description and costs. Fully specified.
- total_budget: requires complete cost breakdown. Provided a breakdown including property price, fees, survey cost, moving, utility, and overall total. Fully specified.
Therefore, all 6 out of 6 items are included and sufficiently specified per the format.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final property solution includes the following selectable items that appear in the ground-truth ratings: Property: Waterfront Property; Financing: Traditional Mortgage; Legal services: Premium Legal Package; Moving services: Premium Moving; Utility setup: Premium Utilities; Property survey: Full Structural Survey (listed as property_survey_cost in the budget). I matched each of these to their corresponding ratings from the ground-truth.
